title: Ringworm Basics
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Ringworm is a common fungal skin infection and can affect body sites including skin, feet, groin, scalp, beard, and nails.
Body ringworm can cause itchy, ring-shaped, scaly rash and is contagious through contact with infected people, animals, or surfaces.
Many skin ringworm infections are treated with antifungal medicines, while scalp or nail involvement often requires clinician-directed oral treatment.
Patients should seek care for extensive, persistent, recurrent, scalp, nail, beard, or infected-appearing disease or high-risk host factors.
